summary.intent: Update sandbox containers to use Node.js 22 instead of Node.js 20, which was being pulled from Ubuntu 25.10's apt repos by default.
summary.outcome: Successfully updated sandbox Dockerfile generation to add the NodeSource 22.x apt repository when nodejs is in the package list, removed npm from default packages (since NodeSource's nodejs bundles it), added tests, and created a PR.

repo: Default sandbox packages are defined in two places: crates/config/src/schema/tools.rs (default_sandbox_packages()) and crates/config/src/template.rs — both must be kept in sync.
sandbox_packages.rs categorizes packages for display purposes and must be updated alongside schema/template changes.
The sandbox Dockerfile is generated by sandbox_image_dockerfile() in crates/tools/src/sandbox/containers.rs.
NodeSource's nodejs package bundles npm, so npm can be removed as a separate dependency when using NodeSource.
Tests for sandbox Dockerfile generation live in crates/tools/src/sandbox/tests/docker_router.rs.
CLAUDE.md references validate.rs as relevant to schema map changes — worth checking when modifying default packages.

code.finding: sandbox_image_dockerfile() is where NodeSource repo setup logic was added — injects apt repo setup when nodejs is in the package list and filters out npm.
default_sandbox_packages() contained both nodejs and npm; npm removed since NodeSource bundles it.
Parallel definition of default packages in config template — must mirror schema/tools.rs changes.
Categorized package display list — npm entry removed to reflect it's bundled with nodejs.
Added tests verifying NodeSource setup appears when nodejs is requested and is absent otherwise; existing tests only used curl so were unaffected.

workflow: After changes, ran tests per-crate (sandbox: 225 tests, config: 247 tests), then clippy, then rustfmt, then re-ran tests to confirm formatting didn't break anything.
Ubuntu 25.10 apt repos ship Node 20; NodeSource is the correct approach to get Node 22 in Debian/Ubuntu environments.

friction: Node version sourcing required understanding the interaction between Ubuntu apt defaults and NodeSource — not immediately obvious from the codebase alone.
npm had to be removed from multiple locations (schema, template, display list) — no single source of truth for default packages.
open_items: No .nvmrc or .node-version files were found to pin Node version at the repo level — host development environment may still default to Node 20.
The Dockerfile at the repo root was read but no changes were made to it — unclear if it also needs Node 22 updates for the host/Moltis install path.
